crossReferencePatternConsistency.hubToOperationPages: The hub page (T-001) references workflows_xp and content_sync_xp directly. It does not directly link to the business-user operation pages (create-pages, delete-pages, edit-and-publish-pages) because the hub is the authoritative reference — the operation pages link TO the hub, not the other way around. This unidirectional pattern is consistent and intentional.
crossReferencePatternConsistency.operationToHub: All 7 non-hub modified pages (T-002 through T-008) reference the hub page (permissions_pagelevel_xp). Anchors used are specific to the relevant operation section: 'Page permissions for publishing pages', 'Page permissions for creating pages', 'Page permissions for unpublishing pages', 'Page permissions for moving pages', 'Permission requirements for page operations', 'Edit page permissions'. All anchors resolve correctly.
crossReferencePatternConsistency.anchorPattern: Cross-references consistently use descriptive link text (e.g., 'page permissions', 'page permission management') with operation-specific anchors. The pattern is uniform across all modified pages.
crossReferencePatternConsistency.inpageLinkConsistency: All inpage_link references (within-page anchors) in all 8 modified pages resolve to existing headings within those pages.

summary.verdict: All cross-references are valid. No updates needed.
